A quoted price tells you which grade you are really buying. At current input costs, genuine Premium-grade bulk shisha charcoal stands at about $1,513/t, Super Premium at $1,563/t and Platinum at $1,662/t — the gap is the extra material lost to reach lower ash. An offer far below the Premium standard is a red flag, not a bargain.

Genuine bulk shisha charcoal stands at about $1,513/t for Premium, $1,563/t for Super Premium and $1,662/t for Platinum grade at current input costs (verified 15 July 2026; factory model).

Platinum grade costs more because about 35% of the raw material is cleaned away for low ash, versus 20% for Premium (verified 15 July 2026).

An offer more than ~5% below the Premium standard (under ≈ $1,437/t bulk) is below the cost of genuine coconut briquette — expect mixed material, chemical binders or short weight (verified 15 July 2026).

Standard factory price per grade at raw material Rp 13,500/kg, Rp 17,000/USD, 25% margin · verified 15 July 2026
GradeMaterial lossBulk (USD/t)+ inner boxes+ inner + colour master
Platinum35%$1,662$1,782$1,842
Super Premium25%$1,563$1,683$1,743
Premium20%$1,513$1,633$1,693

Bulk = production cost only, with margin. Inner boxes add $120/t; a colour-printed master box adds $60/t. Cleaner grades cost more because more material is cleaned away.

How to read a supplier quote

Start from the packaging: a bulk quote and a retail-packed quote are different products. Inner boxes add about $120/t and a colour-printed master box about $60/t, so strip those out before comparing against the grade standards. A quote for a 22 t order at $1,500/t bulk is closest to the Premium standard — if the supplier calls it Platinum, either the spec is not real Platinum or something else in the deal is compensating.

The standards come from the production-cost model: each grade's material loss (35% Platinum, 25% Super Premium, 20% Premium) is applied to the raw material, fixed production costs are added, then a 25% factory margin. They move with the rupiah, the raw-material market and the margin — treat them as a reality check on a quote, and confirm the actual grade with a COA and burn test.

Frequently asked questions

What grade of shisha charcoal does $1,500 per ton buy?

At current input costs, $1,500/t bulk is closest to the Premium standard ($1,513/t). Genuine Platinum stands near $1,662/t bulk, so a $1,500 “Platinum” offer deserves a burn test.

Why is Platinum grade more expensive than Premium?

Not because of marketing — because of yield. Platinum loses about 35% of the raw material to extra rotary cleaning for low ash, versus 20% for Premium, so every finished kilogram consumed more shell.

Is a very cheap shisha charcoal offer a scam?

Below roughly $1,437/t bulk the numbers stop working for genuine coconut briquette at standard spec. Common ways the gap is closed: mixed wood or low-grade material, chemical binders instead of tapioca, higher moisture, or short weight per carton.

Check your quote

Enter the offered price and tick what the quote includes — the checker names the closest grade standard.

Last verified 15 July 2026. Figures are illustrative industry ranges, not quotes — verify before relying on them. Freight and the US–Indonesia tariff change most often.
